setupGuide.notes: No 1978 Monaco layout in AMS2; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the era-closest 1988 layout (modern Azure is the alternative).

Placeholder for Circuit Zolder — 70 laps / 298.34 km reproduced as 43 laps of spa-francorchamps_1993. No AMS2 Zolder; Spa-Francorchamps 1993 keeps the Belgian GP in Belgium on an era-adjacent layout of the country's other grand prix venue.

Placeholder for Circuito del Jarama — 75 laps / 255.3 km reproduced as 61 laps of jerez_1988. No AMS2 Jarama; Jerez 1988 keeps the Spanish GP in Spain with the same tight, technical low-speed character.

Placeholder for Scandinavian Raceway, Anderstorp — 70 laps / 282.17 km reproduced as 60 laps of silverstone_1975. No AMS2 Anderstorp; Silverstone 1975 matches its flat, aerodrome-straight-dominated character and is free in 1978 (the British GP runs at Brands Hatch this season).
Placeholder for Circuit Paul Ricard — 54 laps / 313.686 km reproduced as 75 laps of le_mans_bugatti. No AMS2 Paul Ricard; Le Mans Circuit Bugatti keeps the French GP at a French Grand Prix venue.
AMS2 ships the Österreichring as Spielberg Vintage (1974, pre-Hella-Licht chicane); the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept (the 1977 Historic layout is the era-closest alternative).

Placeholder for Circuit Park Zandvoort — 75 laps / 316.95 km reproduced as 54 laps of spielberg_historic. No AMS2 Zandvoort; the venue's fast old-school sweepers map to the Österreichring — the base Spielberg Vintage stand-in already hosts this season's Austrian GP, so its 1977 Historic sibling takes this round.
No 1978 Monza chicane layout in AMS2; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the era-matching 1971 layout.
AMS2 ships the period Watkins Glen 1971 Grand Prix layout; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept (the modern Watkins Glen is the alternative).
